scope: POST/PATCH/DELETE /taxa, edge endpoints, GET /taxa, GET /trees, taxon store mechanics.

tests.name: GET /taxa: empty store → 200 with empty list
GET /taxa: returns taxa in creation order with the full record shape
GET /taxa/{id}: existing taxon → 200 with full record
GET /taxa/{id}: missing id → 404 with not_found envelope
GET /taxa/{id}: a shared taxon lists both parents in attachment order
GET /trees: empty store → 200 with empty list
GET /trees: two roots with chains under each — both appear with parentIds: []
GET /trees: a previously-root taxon attached under another parent no longer appears
GET /trees/{rootId}: a leaf root → { id, name, owner, children: [] }
GET /trees/{rootId}: two-children root expands them in insertion order
GET /trees/{rootId}: a shared child appears under both roots when each tree is fetched
GET /trees/{id}: non-root existing id → 404 with 'not a root' message
reads: all four endpoints accept the null user (no X-Username)
reads: all four endpoints accept a registered caller
reads: all four endpoints 403 on an unregistered non-null caller
reads: all four endpoints 400 on malformed X-Username (NBSP-prefixed)
reset: clearing wipes taxa AND resets the ID counter to t1
reset: previously-issued taxon ids 404 on /taxa/{id} and /trees/{id}
reset: trees endpoint after reset returns empty
taxa: a freshly-reset store has zero taxa
taxa: getState exposes empty taxa and parentsOf maps after reset
taxa: createTaxonFixture assigns t1, t2, t3 in monotonic order
taxa: each new taxon starts with empty childIds and no parents
taxa: name and owner are stored verbatim (no format enforcement in the store)
taxa: attaching a child puts it on the parent's childIds in attachment order
taxa: the derived parent set lists parents in attachment order
taxa: re-attaching the same edge is idempotent
taxa: detachChildFixture removes the edge from both endpoints
taxa: detachChildFixture on a non-existent edge is a no-op
taxa: attachChildFixture throws on unknown parent or child (fixture-author bug)
taxa: deeply-nested attachments preserve insertion order and per-id distinctness
taxa: clear() empties the store AND resets the ID counter
taxa: clear() removes edges from the parents-of index too
taxa: allTaxa returns taxa in creation order (insertion-ordered Map)
POST /taxa: registered caller creates a root taxon owned by themselves
POST /taxa: consecutive creates issue distinct monotonic IDs
POST /taxa: freshly created taxon appears in reads as a root
POST /taxa: two registered users each create their own taxa, owners distinct
POST /taxa: name format violations → 400 (empty, leading/trailing whitespace)
POST /taxa: unknown body keys (including 'owner') are ignored — caller is owner per §6.1
DELETE taxon: lone root with no children → region {N}, 204
DELETE taxon: root with U-owned children only → cascade deletes everything
DELETE taxon: deep U-owned chain (4+ levels) → all deleted
DELETE taxon: non-root N with one U-owned parent → N's region deleted; parent survives, edge gone
DELETE taxon: single other-owned child at halt point — child not deleted, edge removed, child becomes root
DELETE taxon: halt-frontier child with another parent — survives, retains other parent
DELETE taxon: stranded U-owned descendant below an other-owned halt point — survives (§14 bullet 4)
DELETE taxon: multiple other-owned children at the halt frontier — each detached, edges removed
DELETE taxon: halt-and-detach applies at every level, not just N's children
DELETE taxon: N itself is shared (has two parents) → 409 shared_in_region
DELETE taxon: a U-owned descendant in the region is shared into another tree → 409 shared_in_region
DELETE taxon: a stranded U-owned descendant beneath an other-owned halt point is shared — does NOT trip precondition 1 (it's not in the region)
DELETE taxon: an other-owned halt-frontier taxon is itself shared → 204 (§6.3 parenthetical)
DELETE taxon: N has one parent owned by someone else → 409 parent_not_owned
DELETE taxon: N has multiple parents — at least one precondition fires; tests accept either
DELETE taxon: non-owner caller → 403; no state changes
DELETE taxon: a precondition-failed delete leaves state byte-identical (no partial cascade)
PUT edge: parent owner adds a previously-disconnected child → 204; edge present on both ends
PUT edge: re-adding an existing edge → 204 idempotent, no duplicate entries
PUT edge: child attached under two different trees → both trees show it; child is shared
PUT edge: deeper cycle (attach ancestor under descendant) → 409 cycle
PUT edge: in-tree diamond (child reachable from same root twice) → 409 in_tree_duplicate
PUT edge: in-tree name clash → 409 name_clash
PUT edge: grafting a subtree whose internal name clashes with the receiving tree → 409
PUT edge: same name allowed under two different trees
PUT edge: invariant rejection leaves state byte-identical (no partial edge)
DELETE edge: parent owner detaches a single-parent child → 204; child becomes a root
DELETE edge: detaching a shared child → 204; child retains other parents
DELETE edge: detach preserves the child's subtree (§7 detach ≠ delete)
DELETE edge: both exist but no edge between them → 404 (decision #3)
DELETE edge: post-detach the child is still in /taxa (detach is not delete)
PATCH /taxa/{id}: owner renames → 200 with updated record
PATCH /taxa/{id}: owner reassigns to another registered user — recipient may then act, original cannot
PATCH /taxa/{id}: both name AND owner in one call → both applied atomically
PATCH /taxa/{id}: reassigning owner to self → 200 no-op
PATCH /taxa/{id}: setting name to the current value → 200 no-op (validates trivially)
PATCH /taxa/{id}: case-only name change (Fantasy → FANTASY) → 200, casing updates
PATCH /taxa/{id}: body without name/owner (only unknown keys) → 400
PATCH /taxa/{id}: owner not a string / null / empty / malformed → 400
PATCH /taxa/{id}: unknown keys alongside a valid change are ignored (decision #6)
PATCH /taxa/{id}: rename to a sibling's name in same tree → 409 conflict (name_clash)
PATCH /taxa/{id}: rename to case-variant of sibling → 409 (case-insensitive per §3.4)
PATCH /taxa/{id}: cross-tree rename — valid in 'own' tree, invalid in another containing tree → 409 (the §3.3 / §14 bullet-5 keystone)
PATCH /taxa/{id}: rename that doesn't clash anywhere → 200, visible in every containing tree
PATCH /taxa/{id}: 409 envelope carries kind + offending root/taxa in details
detached child with no other parents becomes a root
DELETE edge: authorization and existence ordering
cascade halts at other-owned child (detached, not deleted)
cascade does NOT continue past halt frontier even into owner-owned descendants
delete of a non-root taxon with a single owner-owned parent succeeds
halt-frontier other-owned taxon that is itself shared elsewhere does NOT trip precondition 1
DELETE taxon: precondition 1 (region contains shared taxon)
region taxon in multiple trees (sibling roots) → 409 delete-region-shared
region taxon shared via ancestor's fan-out → 409 delete-region-shared (disproves parent-count shortcut)
N has a parent owned by another user → 409 delete-parent-not-owned
precondition 1 fires before precondition 2 when both would fail
after a successful delete, no dangling references remain
wraps in object, not bare array
single taxon entry has all six fields
shared:true for taxa in multiple trees; shared:false for roots and unshared
reads do not gatekeep on invariant violations
null user can fetch by id
lists roots with id and name; excludes non-roots
returns single-root tree with empty children
shared taxon appears once per tree with shared:true
Structural — no handler invokes the gate or the invariant engine
unregistered caller can read all four endpoints (sanity for openness)
rename to current name is a no-op success
cross-tree clash surfaces the other root
owner reassignment to a registered user succeeds
after reassignment the prior owner gets 403 on subsequent PATCH
owner reassignment canonicalizes casing to the registry's stored form
null owner → 400 (username-format violation, value not a string)
rename failure rolls back the owner change too
null caller → 403 (no details.tag from this layer)
validation order: unregistered-owner fires before checkRename
validation order: malformed-body fires before owner-registry lookup
registered creator gets 201 with the full serialized taxon
created taxon is visible in GET /taxa, /taxa/{id}, /trees
owner is stored in registered casing regardless of X-Username casing
empty name is the format validator's tag 400
validation order: missing-name before format check
POST /reset clears created taxa and zeros the counter
ten concurrent POSTs yield exactly ten distinct IDs t1..t10
child's subtree contains the parent → 409 cycle
child's subtree contains a taxon already reachable from parent's root → 409 in-tree-duplicate
child's deep descendant has a name clashing with parent's tree (case variant) → 409 in-tree-name-clash
child's subtree shares with another tree (no violation) → 204

creating an in-tree name clash → 409 in-tree-name-clash (case variant)
exposes all six fields including shared:false for a root
marks shared:true for taxa in multiple trees
returns one entry per taxon with full shape
response is internally consistent (every parent/child pair is symmetric)
lists only roots with id and name
returns undefined when the id exists but is not a root
shared taxon appears once per tree with shared:true
mints t1 first on a fresh store and inserts as a root
no-op patch (empty object) returns the current taxon unchanged
throws plain Error on unknown id
stores owner verbatim (no normalization at the store seam)
returns true when an edge existed and was removed
returns false when the edge did not exist
does not throw on unknown parent
does not throw on unknown child
removes region taxa and detaches halt-frontier edges in one call
empty region with empty frontier is a no-op
frontier-only call removes the edges without removing taxa
removes outgoing-only edges of region taxa when frontier not enumerated

seed and createTaxon share the same counter
seed still mints t1 after reset
getById returns undefined on empty store
mints monotonically across taxa in one call
rejects an edge referencing an unknown ID with a plain Error
rejects an edge referencing an unknown parent
accepts pre-existing IDs in subsequent edges

zeroes the ID counter so the next mint is t1
